summary: Matched Dolphin MTX 4x4 projection matrix code by replacing asm/dolphin/mtx/mtx44.s with src/dolphin/mtx/mtx44.c, adding C implementations for C_MTXFrustum, C_MTXPerspective, and C_MTXOrtho. The build object list was switched from the assembly object to the C object, mtx.h gained/normalized prototypes, and tools/convert_data.py was temporarily retargeted to asm/**/mtx44.s. The PR body only says it was tracked in #480 and there were no review comments, so intent beyond matching this asm file is not evidenced.
what_changed: Deleted the 163-line assembly implementation asm/dolphin/mtx/mtx44.s, including C_MTXFrustum, C_MTXPerspective, C_MTXOrtho, and local sdata2 float labels lbl_804DE328 through lbl_804DE33C.
Added src/dolphin/mtx/mtx44.c with C implementations of C_MTXFrustum, C_MTXPerspective, and C_MTXOrtho using Mtx44 indexing and f32 temporaries.
Changed obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES builds $(BUILD_DIR)/src/dolphin/mtx/mtx44.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/dolphin/mtx/mtx44.s.o.
Updated include/dolphin/mtx.h by adding a C_MTXFrustum prototype and normalizing C_MTXOrtho formatting; the include order was adjusted to include mtxtypes before types.
Updated tools/convert_data.py input_glob from asm\melee\ft\*_unknown_006.s to asm\**\mtx44.s, likely to help convert/extract data for this matching target.
smart_moves: Kept the C translation small and direct: the new file maps each matrix element assignment explicitly to m[row][col], making the matched projection formulas readable while staying close to the assembly store order.

Preserved floating-point expression structure with named temporaries such as temp_f11, temp_f8, temp_f9, temp_f6, temp_f4, and temp_f3, which is a common matching tactic when register allocation and operation ordering matter.

Used explicit float constants with F suffixes, including 1.0F, 2.0F, 0.5F, 0.0F, -1.0F, and the degree-to-radian factor 0.017453292F, matching the original single-precision code path and sdata2 constants.
Replaced the object in obj_files.mk in the same PR that added the C file, ensuring the build consumes the matched source instead of leaving the old asm in place.
Added the missing public C_MTXFrustum declaration to the Dolphin MTX header when moving the function into C source.
decomp_lessons: For small math-library assembly files, a literal C implementation with explicit row/column stores can be enough to retire the asm file cleanly.

Projection matrix functions are sensitive to float expression ordering; keeping divisions, negations, and multiplications in the same apparent order as the assembly can help preserve matching codegen.
When an asm file has embedded sdata2 float labels, the equivalent C constants may be generated naturally by writing single-precision constants and expressions rather than manually preserving labels.
Header work should accompany source matching when a previously asm-only function becomes a normal C symbol; here C_MTXFrustum was added to include/dolphin/mtx.h.
Build-system migration is part of the match: switch obj_files.mk from asm/*.s.o to src/*.c.o only once the C source is ready to replace the assembly.
naming_conventions: Dolphin MTX API symbols use the C_MTX prefix and UpperCamel suffixes: C_MTXFrustum, C_MTXPerspective, C_MTXOrtho.
Mtx44 projection function parameters use short axis/plane names in the prototypes and definitions: t, b, l, r, n, f.
The C_MTXPerspective field-of-view parameter is spelled fovY in both header and implementation.
Temporary names in this match intentionally mirror compiler floating-point register names, such as temp_f8, temp_f10, temp_f11, temp_f4, and temp_f3.
Matrix values are expressed through Mtx44 m with two-dimensional indexing, e.g. m[0][0], m[3][3].
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated C_MTXFrustum by computing reciprocal spans first: 1.0F / (r - l), 1.0F / (t - b), and 1.0F / (f - n), then filling all 16 Mtx44 entries explicitly.
Matched C_MTXPerspective with fovY halved before calling tanf on 0.017453292F * fovY, preserving the assembly's tanf call and degree-to-radian conversion constant.
Used temp_f3 = 1.0F / (f - n) and temp_f4 = 1.0F / tanf(...) in C_MTXPerspective to drive the same matrix assignments as the assembly.
Used explicit negative products such as temp_f6 * -(f * n), -n * temp_f3, and -f * temp_f4 rather than algebraically rearranging them, which helps avoid different instruction selection.
C_MTXOrtho was written in phases corresponding to the X, Y, and Z rows: compute reciprocal, assign row entries, then compute the next reciprocal.
follow_up_queries: Find other Dolphin MTX asm files still built from asm/dolphin/mtx/*.s.o in obj_files.mk.
Search for later edits to src/dolphin/mtx/mtx44.c to see if parameter names, constants, or expressions were refined after PR #505.
Check issue or PR #480 for the broader tracking context referenced by this PR.
Look for similar matches of mtx.c, mtxvec.c, or vec.c to reuse expression-order tactics for Dolphin math code.
